function.name: open_app
function.description: Open a macOS application by name. Use for utterances like "open Safari", "launch Notes", "throw on Spotify".

name.description: Canonical macOS application name as it appears in /Applications. Examples: 'Safari', 'Notes', 'Visual Studio Code', 'Spotify'.

function.name: open_url
function.description: Open a URL in the user's default browser. Use when the user names a website or domain without specifying which browser. Examples: 'open hacker news', 'go to gmail', 'open github.com'.

url.description: Full URL including https://. If the user said a bare domain, prepend https://.

function.name: open_url_in_app
function.description: Open a specific URL inside a specific app. Use whenever the user names BOTH a URL/domain AND a browser app. Example: 'open gmail.com in Safari'.

url.description: Full URL including https://. If the user said a bare domain, prepend https://.

app.description: The app name as the user said it. Examples: 'Safari', 'Google Chrome', 'Arc', 'Firefox', 'Brave Browser'.

function.name: search_web
function.description: Open a Google search in the default browser. Use ONLY when the user is asking for fresh / live information they explicitly want searched — current news, today's prices, latest scores. For things you can answer from your own knowledge (capitals, definitions, conversions), reply in text instead — do NOT call this tool.

query.description: A clean Google search query stripped of filler words (no 'can you', 'please', 'open and').

function.name: remember
function.description: Persistently store a short fact about the user so you can recall it in future sessions. Use whenever the user tells you something about themselves they'd want you to remember (their name, preferences, common contacts, default browser, etc.). Rewrite the fact in clean third-person, e.g. "user's name is Vivek" or "user prefers Safari as their browser".

fact.description: A concise factual statement to persist.

function.name: search_files
function.description: Search the user's local filesystem (Documents, Downloads, Desktop) using Spotlight. Use when the user asks to find, locate, or search for a file on their Mac. Returns matching file names. Pass open_top=true if the user also wants to open the best match immediately.

query.description: Spotlight search query. Use plain keywords (e.g. 'resume PDF', 'invoice 2024'). Avoid shell metacharacters.

open_top.description: If true, automatically open the top-matching file after the search.

function.name: system_command
function.description: Run a local macOS system action.
